data.title: As a developer, resource exhaustion is caught as a safety net at commit time
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add EAGAIN detection to record-test-status.sh as a safety net layer that reclassifies EAGAIN failures as resource_exhaustion
**Why**: Suite-engine handles most EAGAIN failures via retry, but tests run directly by record-test-status.sh at commit time bypass suite-engine. This safety net catches those cases.
**Scope**:
- IN: EAGAIN stderr pattern detection in record-test-status.sh, writing resource_exhaustion status instead of failed, deferring to suite-engine retry result when present
- OUT: Suite-engine retry logic (separate story), test gate changes (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, record-test-status.sh applies the EAGAIN grep pattern as a safety net, writing resource_exhaustion status instead of failed when the pattern matches — only when suite-engine has not already retried and written passed
  ← Satisfies: "Record-test-status.sh applies the same EAGAIN grep pattern as a safety net"
- When this story is complete, the safety net defers to suite-engine's retry result — if suite-engine already wrote passed, record-test-status.sh does not overwrite with resource_exhaustion
  ← Satisfies: "When suite-engine retries a test and the retry passes, the retry result (passed) is the authoritative status"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Uses the same EAGAIN regex pattern as suite-engine — consider extracting to a shared constant or function to maintain DRY
